title: Hi, I'm Karthik - LLM & Cloud Security Engineer
excerpt: LLM Engineer and Cloud Security Engineer—models in production, AWS IAM and guardrails, and the overlap between the two. This is my corner of the internet.
content:
# Hi, I'm Karthik

LLM Engineer and Cloud Security Engineer. I care about how language models behave once they leave a notebook—and about the AWS accounts, policies, and logs wrapped around anything you ship.

This is my corner of the internet — a place where I write about what I'm learning, building, and occasionally staying up until 2 AM debugging.

---

## What I Do

### LLM engineering

- **Evals & quality** — defining what "good" means for a feature, measuring it, and catching regressions before users do
- **RAG & context** — retrieval design, chunking, and keeping answers grounded without turning the stack into spaghetti
- **Tooling & agents** — structured outputs, calling APIs safely, and knowing when *not* to give a model a hammer
- **Production hardening** — prompts are code; the stack around models deserves the same discipline as any other service

### Cloud security (AWS)

- **IAM & access control** — least-privilege policies, trust boundaries, and keeping `*:*` far away from production
- **Threat modeling** — mapping attack surfaces before someone else does
- **Security automation** — guardrails so humans aren't the last line of defense
- **Incident response** — CloudTrail, containment, and making sure it doesn't happen again

The two sides reinforce each other: the same instinct that makes you audit IAM makes you audit what a model is allowed to do once it can call tools.

---

## Background

I got into cloud security because the cloud moves fast and gaps show up in permissions and defaults—not just in headlines. I leaned into LLMs because the same pattern repeats: powerful primitives, sharp edges, and a lot of room between a demo and something you'd trust with customer data.

I've worked across cloud infrastructure and backend systems before narrowing on security and then on model-backed products. That builder lens still shapes how I write policies and how I design evals.

---

## What I'm Building

Right now I'm focused on:

- Shipping and measuring model-backed features in real products—not just benchmarks in isolation
- Staying sharp on AWS security—GuardDuty, Security Hub, IAM Access Analyzer, and the boring logs that matter
- Writing up lessons that were painful to learn and thinly documented elsewhere
- Staying connected through this blog and [DEV.to](https://dev.to/karthiblip)

---
